prohibited_actions: Creating new GitHub issues outside this universe
Creating new planning tracks or backlog groups
Expanding scope through 'while we're here' additions
Adding new store types beyond the 5 defined
Adding new system categories not represented in existing issues
allowed_modifications: Updating existing issue scope within its defined boundaries
Marking issues complete, in-progress, or blocked
Adding implementation details to existing issues
Splitting an existing issue into sub-tasks if scope proves too large
Closing issues as won't-fix with documented rationale
Updating design docs to reflect implementation discoveries
what_counts_as_existing_work: Any task that falls within the scope of an existing issue in this universe. If a task is needed but no existing issue covers it, it must be a sub-task of the closest existing issue, not a new top-level issue.
exception_process: If a genuinely missing workstream is discovered that cannot be mapped to any existing issue, document it in planning/reports/ and request explicit scope expansion approval before creating new issues.
